heroPoems.title: The Boston Evening Transcript
heroPoems.firstStanza:
The readers of the Boston Evening Transcript
Sway in the wind like a field of ripe corn.
When evening quickens faintly in the street,
Wakening the appetites of life in some
And to others bringing the Boston Evening Transcript,
I mount the steps and ring the bell, turning

heroPoems.title: Inner Enemy Briefing
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Name the adversary: craving.
Its camouflage: pleasure.
Its appetite: endless.
heroPoems.tagline: For when rain taps the window.

heroPoems.title: Do not go to the garden of flowers!
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Do not go to the garden of flowers!
O Friend! go not there;
In your body is the garden of flowers.
Take your seat on the thousand petals of the lotus, and there
gaze on the Infinite Beauty.

heroPoems.title: Set Me as a Seal
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Set me as a seal upon your heart,
as a seal upon your arm,
for love is strong as death,
passion fierce as the grave.
heroPoems.tagline: For when rain taps the window.

heroPoems.title: Yet Do I Marvel
heroPoems.firstStanza:
I doubt not God is good, well-meaning, kind,
And did He stoop to quibble could tell why
The little buried mole continues blind,
Why flesh that mirrors Him must someday die,
Make plain the reason tortured Tantalus
Is baited by the fickle fruit, declare

heroPoems.title: When I go from hence let this be
heroPoems.firstStanza:
When I go from hence let this be my parting word, that what I have seen
is unsurpassable.
I have tasted of the hidden honey of this lotus that expands on the
ocean of light, and thus am I blessed—let this be my parting word.
In this playhouse of infinite forms I have had my play and here have I
caught sight of him that is formless.
heroPoems.tagline: For when rain taps the window.

heroPoems.title: I heard a fly buzz when I died
heroPoems.firstStanza:
I heard a fly buzz when I died;
The stillness round my form
Was like the stillness in the air
Between the heaves of storm.
heroPoems.tagline: For rooms lit by one lamp.

heroPoems.title: The rose opened and the nightingale went drunk with joy
heroPoems.firstStanza:
The rose opened and the nightingale went drunk with joy
Wine was announced to a thirsty world
Repentance looked like a mountain
one small cup split it cleanly in two

heroPoems.title: The Winners
heroPoems.firstStanza:
What is the moral? Who rides may read.
When the night is thick and the tracks are blind
A friend at a pinch is a friend indeed,
But a fool to wait for the laggard behind.
Down to Gehenna or up to the Throne,
He travels the fastest who travels alone.
heroPoems.tagline: For when rain taps the window.

heroPoems.title: Fog
heroPoems.firstStanza:
The fog comes
on little cat feet.
heroPoems.tagline: For slow hours with a warm cup.

collections.name: The Nearness of Distant Days
collections.tagline: For when sleep arrives in fragments.
collections.theme: Poems that move through corridors, stairwells, and rooms as emotional architecture.

featuredPoem.title: Order of Battle
featuredPoem.firstLines:
Princes, teachers, cousins, kings—
all arranged in perfect lines

poems.title: Knowing the Vast and the Near
A bird came down the walk

collections.name: A Corridor of Weather
collections.tagline: For rooms heavy with old light.
collections.theme: A restrained current of longing that never announces itself directly.

featuredPoem.title: The Stranger
featuredPoem.firstLines:
The Stranger within my gate,
He may be true or kind.

poems.title: O my heart! the Supreme Spirit, the great Master, is near you
To Thee Thou hast drawn my love, O Fakir!

collections.name: What Remains Unsaid
collections.tagline: For when language is your company.
collections.theme: Poems for the slow pivot from day-noise toward interior quiet.

featuredPoem.title: Lamps burn in every house, O blind one! and you cannot see them.
featuredPoem.firstLines:
Lamps burn in every house, O blind one! and you cannot see them.
One day your eyes shall suddenly be opened, and you shall see:

poems.title: Why did I laugh tonight? No voice will tell
Set my glass on fire with wine
